text: Klopp is finished at Liverpool

Real Madrid's defensive recovery rate improved 22% after adding Tchouameni—specifically at winning possession in the attacking third. This tactical change enables more counter-attacks, evidenced by their 2.1 transition goals per game (up from 1.4).
Comparing goalkeeper sweeper actions per game: Alisson (4.2), Mendy (3.8), De Gea (2.1). These differences correlate with team possession and passing style rather than pure shot-stopping ability.

Chelsea's attacking transitions improved after signing Enzo: his 94% pass completion on progressive passes (vs 87% for his predecessor) enables faster attacks. The data suggests fit-for-system impact.

Sevilla's defensive corners strategy: man-marking in near post (0-6m), zonal in far post (6-12m). Success rate: 88% clean sheet vs 73% pure zonal. The hybrid approach mitigates both attack types.

Arsenal's inverted winger system (Saka right, Martinelli left) creates overloads on one flank while leaving the other exposed. This asymmetric approach works well against balanced defenses but struggles vs asymmetric systems.

Van Dijk's positioning has evolved: fewer interceptions (5.2/90) but higher tackle success (78% vs 71% average). This suggests a shift toward body positioning over reactive defending, improving efficiency.
The transition game was lightning quick today

Comparing pressing height: Man City (35m from goal), Liverpool (38m), Arsenal (32m). These differences reflect tactical philosophy more than defensive quality.

Liverpool's 4-2-3-1 vs 4-3-3 usage depends on opponent setup. Against high-press teams, the 4-2-3-1 provides midfield stability; against possession-heavy sides, the 4-3-3 offers better ball progression. Their choice of formation correlates with opponent press intensity.
What a hit from outside the box!

Jude Bellingham's assist rate at Borussia Dortmund (0.28 per 90) didn't transfer to Real Madrid early season (0.12 per 90). Context: lower volume in attacking situations (4.2 vs 6.1 per 90), not reduced quality.
Brighton's high press triggers on 70% of possessions but has only 45% success rate. This aggressive approach generates turnovers but overexposures defensive shape.

Mbappé's dribble success rate (0.62) vs Neymar's (0.58) needs context: Mbappé attempts more dribbles on the wing (8.2/90), while Neymar attempts fewer but from more congested areas (6.1/90). Volume-adjusted, their efficiency is closer.

Comparing penalty takers by pressure: Salah (penalty xG: 0.79, conversion: 89%), De Bruyne (0.79, conversion: 92%), Lewandowski (0.78, conversion: 91%). Elite penalties converge at ~90% despite psychological pressure.
